heroHeadline: Sheinbaum at 567 Days: Harfuch Formally Requests Chihuahua Explain CIA Incident; Inflation Eases to 4.53%; Semana Santa Sets Tourism Record
heroSubtitle: President Sheinbaum's April 24 mañanera advanced the Chihuahua sovereignty crisis: Security Secretary Omar García Harfuch formally requested that Governor Maru Campos explain the unauthorized CIA operation that killed two U.S. agents and two Mexican AEI investigators on April 20 — Campos agreed to provide the information. Sheinbaum ruled out a personal meeting with Campos and reiterated that all foreign security cooperation must route through federal channels, while stressing the incident 'does not rupture bilateral relations.' On the economic front, INEGI data showed annual inflation eased to 4.53% in the first half of April (from 4.63% in March), helped by electricity subsidies (-14%) partially offsetting soaring tomato prices (+24.37%); the peso softened further to 17.42 MXN/USD amid global geopolitical headwinds. Separately, Tourism Secretary Josefina Rodríguez presented record Semana Santa figures — 7.82 million visitors and 30.74 billion pesos in economic activity — ahead of the June 11 FIFA World Cup opening. Sheinbaum also announced the arrest in Buenos Aires of former Rear Admiral Fernando Farías Laguna, accused of leading a huachicol (fuel theft) network, with extradition proceedings to follow.
footerNote: Data sourced from Diario Oficial, INEGI, Banco de México, SNSP, Secretaría de Economía, INAH, Reuters, AP, El Universal, Reforma, Animal Político, Mexico News Daily, Infobae, Milenio, La Jornada, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, AtlasIntel, USTR, Chatham House, Mexico Business News, El Financiero, La Silla Rota, Proceso, CNN Español, Lopez Doriga, The Yucatan Times, Excelsior, La Razón de México, Quinto Poder, PBS NewsHour, NBC News, Time, NPR, CBC, Japan Kantei, Japan MOFA, SDP Noticias, Eje Central, TVAzteca, UnoTV, CBS News, El Mañana de Nuevo Laredo, Bloomberg Línea
